01 Overview & Recommendation

Snapshot of where things stand ahead of opening the next Letter of Credit for FCAW E71T‑1 welding wire, and what changed versus the last shipment.

Decision framing

Proceed with the LC — but renegotiate before opening

  1. Both SKUs are on "Immediately Buy" procurement action with stock selling out within this month — there is no room to delay sourcing.
  2. The new offer price is 1.7% higher than the last LC ($0.90/$0.89 vs $0.885/$0.875 per kg), and Regulatory Duty has moved from 3% → 5%, pushing landed cost/kg up 3.2% and compressing gross margin from 14.5% → 11.8%.
  3. Even so, projected EAT on this lot (৳171k) still beats last LC's booked EAT (৳127k) — the plan remains net accretive, largely because sales price is fixed regardless of cost.
  4. Industry data shows Electrofab is still buying below the market average price every month sampled — use this as leverage to hold supplier CFR near $0.87–0.88/kg on the next round.

Reading: the entire in‑hand stock (1,372 packets / ~20,580kg) is planned to clear within July. With zero incoming stock booked and the last LC's wire the only pipeline cover, the new LC needs to open now to avoid a stock‑out before the ~30‑day shipment window closes.

Source: "P&L Impact" and "Last LC Performance" sheets, blended (BDT) column. Gross Sales Revenue is planned/fixed regardless of sourcing cost, so margin compression from higher landed cost shows up first in Gross Profit; SG&A allocation swings (last LC carried heavier Admin/Selling load) are why EBITDA and EAT actually come out ahead on the current offer despite the thinner gross margin.

05 Landed Cost & Tariff Breakdown

HS Code 8311.20.00. The source workbook shows an explicit "Current Offer" duty structure of CD 25% / RD 3% / SD 0% / VAT 15% / AIT 5% / AT 8%. Back‑calculating the same ratios from the Last LC's actual figures shows every rate held steady except Regulatory Duty, which moved from 3% to 5% — that is the tariff change driving this LC's higher landed cost.

Landed cost buildup — Last LC ৳ per 49,680kg lot

CFR Value5,413,000
Customs Duty (CD) — 25%1,717,418
Regulatory Duty (RD) — 3%206,090
Port, C&F, transport, insurance, LC charges260,662
Landed Cost (= COGS)7,597,168

Landed cost buildup — Current Offer ৳ per 49,680kg lot

CFR Value5,506,166
Customs Duty (CD) — 25%1,717,501
Regulatory Duty (RD) — 5%343,500
Port, C&F, transport, insurance, LC charges269,694
Landed Cost (= COGS)7,836,862

Effective duty & tax rates

DutyLast LCCurrent OfferStatus
Customs Duty (CD, on assessable value)25.0%25.0%Unchanged
Regulatory Duty (RD, on assessable value)3.0%5.0%+2.0pp
Supplementary Duty (SD)0%0%Unchanged
VAT (effective, on AV+CD+RD)15.08%15.00%Unchanged
AIT (on assessable value)5.0%5.0%Unchanged
AT (effective, on AV+CD+RD+VAT)6.52%6.52%Unchanged
Cost of the RD change: holding assessable value roughly flat, the 2pp RD increase alone adds ≈ ৳137,400 (~$1,115) to the landed cost of a 49,680kg lot — about 40% of the total landed‑cost increase versus the last LC.

Landed cost / kg trend

VAT, AIT and AT (৳2.33m last LC / ৳2.35m current) are paid in cash at customs but are adjustable against output VAT / income tax, so they sit outside landed cost (COGS) — shown here for cash‑flow awareness, not margin impact.

06 Industry Import Data — Last 6 Months

Bangladesh customs import records for FCAW welding wire (HS 8311.20), country of origin China, Jan–Jun 2026. Compares Electrofab's booked import price each month against the market average, and the resulting dollar savings.

Electrofab price vs. market average

Monthly import volume by company (kg)

Savings vs. industry average price

MonthAEFL Qty (kg)AEFL PriceMarket AvgSaved ($)
Jan‑2624,840$0.81$0.836651
Jun‑2649,680$0.88$0.9041,169
Total sampled74,520——1,820
"Saved by AEFL" row from source pivot — Electrofab bought below the market‑wide monthly average in both sampled months, even though the market average itself rose ~8% from January to June.

Importer directory 6‑month sample

CompanyTotal Qty (kg)Price range
Market‑wide monthly total (all importers, per pivot "Industry" row) ranged $0.82–$0.93/kg, trending upward through H1 2026.
Read‑through for negotiation: the market average climbed steadily from ~$0.836/kg in January to ~$0.904/kg in June — roughly the same +8% drift reflected in this supplier's own offer step‑up. Electrofab's historic discipline in buying below the average price supports pushing back on the $0.90/$0.89 offer toward the $0.87–$0.88/kg range before opening the LC.
